name: Malaysian Stir-Fried Beef with Vegetables
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the steak on a flat surface and cut against the grain into slices about 2 inches wide.
2. Slice each piece of steak horizontally into 2 or 3 pieces.
3. Stack the slices and cut into 1/4-inch strips.
4. Add cornstarch to the steak and toss to coat.
5. Place the steak in a bowl and sprinkle over sugar and soy sauce. Set aside.
6. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a wok or large skillet.
7. Add cauliflower, green pepper, and carrot. Cook, stirring until crisp-tender.
8. Add mushrooms and cook, stirring about 30 seconds longer.
9. Add bean sprouts and stir-fry for a few seconds longer.
10. Remove the vegetables with a slotted spoon and set aside.
11. Add remaining oil to the wok or skillet.
12. When hot, add ginger and garlic and cook, stirring for a few seconds.
13. Add steak and cook, shaking the wok and stirring strips to separate them and cook evenly about 1-2 minutes.
14. Return vegetables to wok and toss quickly over high heat.
15. Serve.
